channel.title: AISight — AI Capital Expenditure Tracker
channel.description: Latest news and data on AI infrastructure spending across 38 companies. Updated 3x daily from SEC filings and earnings calls.

item.title: Tesla Raises 2026 Capital Expenditure to Over $25 Billion, Shares Fall
item.description: Introduction Tesla Inc. shares fell sharply Thursday, April 23, after the electric vehicle maker announced a significant increase in its capital expenditure plans for 2026 – raising investor concerns about its spending on unproven technology platforms. The co… [Tesla]

item.title: Google's $15 billion data centre in Vizag to host 5-GW capacity
item.description: Google Cloud is building a massive AI-ready data centre in Vishakhapatnam. It will have a capacity of 5 gigawatts. This investment is Google's largest in India. The project will be completed over five years. The new campus will be part of Google's global netw… [Alphabet]

item.title: $40 Billion from Google. $25 Billion from Amazon. Anthropic is now the most fought-over AI company on Earth
item.description: Google has committed up to $40 billion to Anthropic, days after Amazon pledged $25 billion, confirming the Claude maker as the AI industry's most coveted startup. [Amazon, Anthropic, Alphabet, ServiceNow]

item.title: Kazakhstan’s digital leap: How Central Asia’s tech hub is shaping the future of AI and GovTech
item.description: Kazakhstan is emerging as a key digital hub in Central Asia, backed by a broad national push in artificial intelligence, digital governance, connectivity and startup development. The country has rolled out major reforms and infrastructure upgrades to position… [Intel]

item.title: Related Digital secures financing for $16 billion Oracle data centre in Michigan
item.description: Related Digital has secured $16 billion in financing for its Michigan data centre campus, a project for Oracle. The funding includes equity from Related Digital and Blackstone, alongside long-term debt financing anchored by PIMCO-managed funds. This developme… [Oracle]

item.title: US tech giant Microsoft announces voluntary retirement: What is it, who is eligible and what is the math used in calculation?
item.description: Microsoft's voluntary retirement: US tech company Microsoft has announced its first-ever voluntary retirement program and the scheme is open to around 7% of its US workforce. If an employee's age and years of service must add up to 70, then he/she is eligible… [Microsoft]

item.title: Anthropic says Google to pump $40 bil into AI startup
item.description: Google is planning to invest up to $40 billion in Anthropic, the artificial intelligence firm confirmed Friday, expanding a long-standing alliance between the two companies. The investment builds on a partnership in which Anthropic will use custom Google chip… [Intel, Anthropic, Alphabet]
